status: backend implemented 2026-07-08 (WP4-A): recommendation_policy read/write + compile-rubric/activate-rubric in backend/app/api/routes/recommendations.py, L3 listwise rerank engine in backend/app/recommendations/rerank.py, purpose buckets in backend/app/llm/budget.py, feedback_reaggregate_daily in backend/app/recommendations/reaggregate.py + workers/scheduler.py (migration f1a2b3c4d5e6); covered by backend/tests/test_recommendation_rerank.py + test_recommendation_policy.py. ordering_consistency/empty_metrics surfaces implemented 2026-07-08 (WP4-E): dashboard headline candidates today-only top6 final_score desc in DashboardPage.vue, GET /api/dedupe-groups default sort=score_desc with news_item_id asc tie-break in backend/app/api/routes/news.py, empty-metric hiding in DailyReportsPage/RecommendationsPage/NewsPage; guarded by DashboardPage/NewsPage/RecommendationsPage/DailyReportsPage specs + backend/tests/test_news_api.py. L2 semantic layer stays interface-reserved and disabled by default.
description: AI recommendation core: three-layer ranking pipeline (rule coarse ranking -> optional semantic layer -> LLM listwise rerank), workspace recommendation_policy with editorial-guidance rubric compilation, final_score fusion, ordering consistency, budget gates and degradation paths. Design doc: docs/backend/recommendation-scoring-design.md
core_rule: LLM rerank only produces ranking signals. It never changes admission_level/admission_pool/noise_types/reject_reasons, never writes raw_items/news_items/dedupe_groups, and never blocks the pipeline: any failure, disabled policy or exhausted budget degrades to pure coarse ranking (final_score = coarse_score). When recommendation_policy is absent or llm_rerank_enabled=false, item ordering must be bit-for-bit identical to the current rule-only implementation (regression red line).

l1_coarse.rule: Current rule/keyword scoring is retained unchanged as the coarse layer and behavioral baseline.
l1_coarse.coarse_score_formula: coarse_score = admission_score*0.35 + quality_score*0.15 + topic_score*0.20 + freshness_score*0.10 + source_score*0.10 + heat_score*0.10 + feedback_score*0.05 + diversity_score; caps: admission=R -> min(coarse_score,25), admission=P3 -> min(coarse_score,44)
l1_coarse.source_prior: source_score adds source_prior_delta from the latest source_score_snapshots row (0 when no snapshot exists, preserving current behavior)

l2_semantic.activation: EMBEDDING_ENABLED=true at instance level AND recommendation_policy.semantic_layer_enabled=true AND provider probe succeeds; otherwise zero calls, zero table writes
uses: near-duplicate advisory: cosine >= 0.92 within same workspace+day_key recorded in scorer_breakdown_json.semantic_duplicate_of; never mutates dedupe_groups
topic clustering (greedy, threshold 0.85) recorded in run summary_json.semantic_clusters for diversity and UI grouping
l2_semantic.env_family: EMBEDDING_ENABLED / EMBEDDING_BASE_URL / EMBEDDING_API_KEY(_REF) / EMBEDDING_MODEL / EMBEDDING_DIM; secret rules identical to GENERATION_API_KEY (credential_ref, startup fail-fast, redaction, never in DB/Git/sync/API)

l3_llm_rerank.trigger: llm_rerank_enabled=true AND rubric_status=active AND generation provider usable AND rerank budget not exhausted
l3_llm_rerank.input_set: top-M candidates by coarse_score desc among admission_level in (P0,P1,P2); R and P3 never enter rerank

window.partition: candidates ordered by coarse_score desc as c1..cM; window k covers indices [k*stride, k*stride+W-1] until all M covered; last window may be short
window.shuffle: in-window prompt order shuffled deterministically with seed sha256(run_key + ':' + window_index); output aligned by candidate id, never by position

l3_llm_rerank.calibration: delta_0=0; for k>=1 delta_k = mean(prev_calibrated_anchor_score - raw_anchor_score) (carry delta_{k-1} if anchors missing or previous window failed); calibrated = clamp(round(raw + delta_k), 0, 100); anchor items keep the score from their first window
l3_llm_rerank.failure_policy: JSON parse failure or id mismatch -> retry same window once (retry counts against budget); still failing -> window items llm_rerank_status=window_failed and fall back to coarse; failed windows > 1/2 of total -> run llm_rerank.status=failed and all items fall back
l3_llm_rerank.hard_exclusion_effect: rubric exclusion with severity=hard hit -> llm_relevance_score capped at 20; admission_level unchanged
l3_llm_rerank.result_cache: same news_item_id + rubric_version + prompt_version scored within 7 days is reused as llm_rerank_status=cached with zero calls
l3_llm_rerank.drift_monitoring: run summary records llm_score_mean/llm_score_std; std<5 -> low_variance=true; |mean - mean(last 7 scored runs)|>15 -> drift_alert=true; v1 alerts only, no score reshaping

final_score_fusion.weights_constraint: each in 0..1 and sum must equal 1.0 (tolerance 0.001), otherwise PATCH answers 422

field_rules: guidance.want/avoid/boost: str <= 2000 chars each; natural-language editorial guidance (what we want / what we do not want / bonus signals).
active_rubric: null or rubric JSON matching rubric_schema; writable only via the activate-rubric action, never via plain PATCH.
rubric_version: int >= 0, incremented by 1 on each activation; 0 means never activated.
rubric_status: none|active (previews are not persisted into the policy).
llm_rerank_enabled: bool, default false; false forces final_score = coarse_score (regression baseline).
rerank_top_m: int 10..200; rerank_window_size: int 6..20; daily_rerank_call_budget: null|int 1..500 (null = unlimited, not recommended).
Out-of-range values answer 422; payloads hitting secret-like detection (privacy.py) answer 422; every write is audited as workspace.recommendation_policy.update with before/after snapshots.
Policies never leak across workspaces; rubric topic codes are never written to generated_news.category or company SQL category (planning_intel stays on the 10 AI categories).

fields.topics: 3..12 items of {code: ^[a-z0-9_]{2,32}$ unique, label: str, weight: float 0.0..5.0, keywords_hint: [str]}
fields.exclusions: 0..10 items of {code, rule: natural language, severity: hard|soft}; hard hit caps llm_relevance_score at 20
fields.scoring_dimensions: must include relevance; codes from relevance|evidence|impact|timeliness|actionability; weights sum to 1.0

rubric_schema.validation: compiled output is strictly validated: unknown keys stripped, missing required keys reject the compile result (retry once, then 502)

rubric_compile.idempotent_preview: same guidance compiles to the same fingerprint and rubric; a cache hit in recommendation_rubric_compiles returns the stored artifact with zero model calls and never changes active_rubric or rubric_version
rubric_compile.budget: compile calls are metered as generation_daily_usage purpose=rubric_compile with a fixed non-configurable cap of 20 per workspace per day

rubric_activate.rule: fingerprint must match a compile record of this workspace created within 7 days, otherwise 422; on success active_rubric is replaced, rubric_version += 1, rubric_status=active

planning_intel.want: AI 工程能力与基础设施、模型训练/推理与服务加速、智能体平台、AI/通算硬件与芯片、核心网与通信系统架构、标准与产业联盟进展、厂商技术路线
planning_intel.avoid: 融资/财报/股价等纯商业新闻、消费电子与个人数码、活动报名与营销宣传、航天火箭等离题工程、生物医学与纯学术离题论文、法律/版权元讨论、标题党与未经证实爆料
planning_intel.boost: 一手技术证据（论文/官方工程博客/benchmark）、架构与成本/能耗/性能量化数据、开源可复现产物、厂商官方技术白皮书
planning_intel.compatibility: llm_rerank_enabled defaults to false, so planning_intel ordering without explicit opt-in stays identical to the current rule-only behavior

ordering_consistency.rule: final_score desc is the single ordering key for every candidate-facing surface; ties broken by news_item_id asc (stable)

surfaces.dashboard_headline_candidates: set = latest run candidates with day_key=today and admission in (P0,P1,P2), top 6 by final_score desc; the current today-first mixed sort is abolished (non-today candidates simply do not enter the set)
surfaces.candidate_pool: GET /api/news-items/dedupe-groups default sort becomes score_desc (final_score desc); other sorts only on explicit selection
surfaces.daily_report_selection: daily draft item sort_order is assigned by final_score desc over the selected set
surfaces.today_overview: all candidate-type cards on the dashboard order by final_score desc

ordering_consistency.empty_metrics: score-like metrics with no data must be hidden, never rendered as zero: rating_count=0 hides the average-rating metric entirely (current '0.0 平均评分' is a violation); missing final_score renders as 未评分, not 0.0
ordering_consistency.frontend_guard: assertions live in DashboardPage.spec.ts / NewsPage.spec.ts / DailyReportsPage.spec.ts / RecommendationsPage.spec.ts

accounting.change: add column purpose String(24) default 'generation'; unique constraint becomes (workspace_code, day_key, purpose); existing rows backfilled purpose='generation'

purposes.rerank: quota from recommendation_policy.daily_rerank_call_budget (default 60); every window call and retry counts, success or failure
purposes.rubric_compile: fixed cap 20 per workspace per day, not configurable

accounting.isolation: the three purpose buckets never consume each other's quota

degradation_paths.behavior: scored windows kept, remaining fall back to coarse
window items fall back to coarse
all items fall back to coarse

budget_and_degradation.non_blocking_rule: no degradation path may block the recommendation run or daily draft creation

recommendation_items_new_columns: coarse_score Float default 0.0 (backfill existing rows with final_score)
data_model_deltas.recommendation_runs_summary_json_keys: llm_rerank: {status: scored|partial|skipped|disabled|failed, skip_reason, windows_total, windows_failed, calls_used, rubric_version, llm_score_mean, llm_score_std, low_variance, drift_alert}
new_tables: recommendation_rubric_compiles(id, workspace_code, fingerprint unique-with-workspace, guidance_json, rubric_json, prompt_version, model_called, created_by, created_at)

source_score_snapshots(id, workspace_code, data_source_id, window, recommended_count, adopted_count, rejected_count, like_count, adopt_rate, reject_rate, like_rate, source_prior_delta, day_key, computed_at; unique(workspace_code, data_source_id, window, day_key))
rubric_topic_priors(id, workspace_code, rubric_version, topic_code, pos_count, neg_count, effective_weight, day_key, computed_at; unique(workspace_code, rubric_version, topic_code, day_key))
news_item_embeddings(id, news_item_id, model, dim, vector_json, created_at; unique(news_item_id, model); written only when L2 enabled)

feedback_reestimation.job: feedback_reaggregate_daily at 02:00 Asia/Shanghai over a trailing 14-day window; idempotent (same day_key rerun overwrites that day's snapshots)
feedback_reestimation.source_prior_formula: adopt_rate = adopted(adoption_status=2 in published reports)/max(1,recommended); reject_rate = rejected(adoption_status=3)/max(1,recommended); like_rate = min(1, likes/max(1,recommended)); source_prior_delta = clamp(8*adopt_rate - 6*reject_rate + 2*like_rate, -6.0, +6.0)
feedback_reestimation.topic_weight_formula: effective_weight = clamp(authored_weight * (1 + 0.1*(pos-neg)/max(5,pos+neg)), 0.5*authored_weight, 1.5*authored_weight); counters reset when rubric_version changes
feedback_reestimation.anti_oscillation: full re-estimation daily (never cumulative); deltas and multipliers are hard-bounded; authored rubric weights are never rewritten
prohibitions: recommendation module never mutates comments, notifications or strategy-loop state
feedback never writes back into the authored rubric or guidance

feedback_workflow.status: implemented (2026-07-08 feedback-loop round, work package WP4-G; verified against backend/app/recommendations/rollup.py, app/workers/scheduler.py, app/api/routes/recommendations.py, migration f3c4d5e6f7a8 and tests test_feedback_rollup.py / test_rubric_proposals.py). Extends the implemented daily re-estimation layer (feedback_reestimation) with weekly/monthly cadences; it never replaces or alters the daily formulas. Design doc: docs/backend/feedback-heat-scoring.md §11-§18.
feedback_workflow.core_rule: Weekly and monthly layers never mutate scores directly: the only score-affecting paths remain the daily job's source_score_snapshots and rubric_topic_priors (formulas and hard bounds unchanged). Weekly/monthly outputs are evaluation snapshots (feedback_rollups), advisory suggestions (source tier / stale-source cleanup, never auto-applied) and rubric revision proposals that only take effect through human review via the existing compile+activate versioned chain. The authored rubric and guidance are never rewritten automatically. With default policy (exploration_epsilon=0) selection and ordering behavior is bit-for-bit identical to the current implementation.

field_rules: weekly_rollup_enabled / monthly_review_enabled / proposal_generation_enabled: bool; rollups are pure snapshots (no score impact) so they default on; proposal generation additionally requires rubric_status=active.
exploration_epsilon: float 0..0.1, default 0.0 (recommended opt-in value 0.05); out-of-range PATCH answers 422; epsilon=0 keeps selection identical to current behavior (regression red line).
Same PATCH endpoint, secret-like detection, audit (workspace.recommendation_policy.update) and cross-workspace isolation rules as the rest of recommendation_policy.

signals.note: Aggregation weights for evaluation, exemplar selection and suggestion ranking only — never a scoring formula (zero direct score mutation). All surfaces verified against backend/app/models/.

list.dedup: once per (requirement_id, news_item_id); strongest one kept per news_item
once per (news_item, daily_report); latest report wins across reports

list.source: weekly_report_items.adoption_status=2 with published report, traced back to news_item
reactions(reaction_type=like, active=true), news_item and daily_report_item dimensions merged

list.dedup: latest per user x target, averaged per news_item before polarity

list.weight: 0 in v1 (observation-only edit_rate metric; polarity is ambiguous without labels; revisit after >=8 weeks of data)

signals.scope: only items that entered recommendation (recommendation_items exists) within the window; event time is the feedback action's created_at; workspaces never cross-contaminate

position_bias_normalization.rank_source: daily_report_items.sort_order (assigned by final_score desc); fallback: recommendation run rank for items that never entered a draft
position_bias_normalization.formula: normalized_adopt_rate = min(1.0, sum(bucket_weight * adopted_in_bucket) / max(1, recommended_count))
position_bias_normalization.usage: weekly evaluation metrics and source tier suggestions only; the daily source_prior formula keeps the un-normalized adopt_rate and source_score_snapshots are never written by this layer

cadence.daily: feedback_reaggregate_daily unchanged (see feedback_reestimation block; implemented in WP4-A)

weekly.job: feedback_weekly_rollup, Monday 03:00 Asia/Shanghai (after the 02:00 daily job so it reads fresh snapshots)
weekly.scheduler_integration: reuses the instance-level fixed-time dispatch pattern of feedback_reaggregate_daily in backend/app/workers/scheduler.py (heartbeat dedup per trigger point, no catch-up after restart, skipped when capability_ingestion is false); the job itself iterates enabled workspaces and one workspace failure never aborts the rest
weekly.period_key: ISO week, e.g. 2026-W28; window = previous complete ISO week (Mon..Sun, Asia/Shanghai)
weekly.idempotency: same (workspace, period_type, period_key) rerun overwrites the same feedback_rollups row (unique constraint); heartbeat dedup guards cross-instance double dispatch
outputs: source tier re-estimation suggestions (suggest_promote: recommended>=8 and normalized_adopt_rate>=0.25 and reject_rate<=0.1; suggest_demote: recommended>=8 and adopted=0 and reject_rate>=0.5; insufficient_data: recommended<5 -> low-data list feeding exploration; advisory only, never mutates data_sources tier/enabled or workspace_source_links.source_weight)

topic rebalance observation: per-topic pos/neg and daily effective-weight series; topics pinned at the daily clamp boundary (>=1.45w or <=0.55w for 7 consecutive days) are listed as authored-weight revision suggestions feeding the proposal prompt; rubric_topic_priors stays written exclusively by the daily job
evaluation report stored in feedback_rollups.metrics_json (see evaluation_metrics)

monthly.job: feedback_monthly_review, 1st of month 03:30 Asia/Shanghai, same dispatch/idempotency/capability rules; zero LLM calls in v1
monthly.period_key: calendar month, e.g. 2026-06; window = previous calendar month
outputs: long-term drift detection: monthly precision_at_6 relative drop >20% and absolute drop >=0.05 vs previous month -> metrics_json.drift_flag=true; plus counts of run-level drift_alert/low_variance; flag only, no reshaping

stale source cleanup suggestions: enabled sources with 4 consecutive weekly rollups at recommended_count=0, or monthly recommended>=8 and adopted=0 and reject_rate>=0.5 -> stale_source_suggestions (suggest_disable|suggest_review); never auto-disables any source
monthly aggregate of weekly metrics and proposal generation/review counts

evaluation_metrics.precision_at_k: K in {6, 12}; per published daily report in window: |top-K by sort_order with adoption_status=2| / min(K, item count), averaged over reports; null (never 0) when the window has no published reports
evaluation_metrics.rerank_uplift: on candidates with llm_rerank_status in (scored, cached): precision@6 under final_score ordering minus precision@6 under coarse_score ordering against the same adoption facts; null when no scored candidates
evaluation_metrics.source_coverage: unique sources among adopted items / adopted count; null when nothing adopted
evaluation_metrics.topic_entropy: Shannon entropy over rubric_hits_json of adopted items normalized by log(topic count), 0..1; only when rubric active, else null
evaluation_metrics.normalized_adopt_rate: position-bias-normalized adoption rate (see position_bias_normalization); null when nothing recommended
evaluation_metrics.edit_rate: share of adopted items with editor overrides (observation-only)
evaluation_metrics.low_data_sources: enabled sources with recommended_count < 5 in window (id/name list)
evaluation_metrics.empty_sample_rule: metrics with no samples are stored as null and must be hidden by the frontend, never rendered as 0.0 (same empty-metric rule as ordering_consistency)

rubric_revision_proposals.trigger: rubric_status=active AND feedback_workflow.proposal_generation_enabled AND (daily adopts + daily rejects in window) >= 10 AND no pending_review proposal exists for the workspace AND generation provider usable (resolve_generation_config chain) AND purpose=feedback_rollup budget available; any miss -> feedback_rollups.proposal_status records skipped_no_rubric|skipped_disabled|skipped_low_data|skipped_pending_exists|skipped_provider|skipped_budget and the rollup still succeeds
rubric_revision_proposals.exemplars: deterministic: adopted items top 8 by final_score desc, rejected items top 8 by final_score desc (high-scored rejects carry the most information); each truncated to {title, summary<=200 chars, source_name, rubric_hits}
rubric_revision_proposals.output: strict JSON {proposed_rubric: full rubric matching rubric_schema, change_summary: [{op, target_code, from, to, rationale<=60 chars}]}; op enum add_topic|remove_topic|adjust_topic_weight|add_exclusion|remove_exclusion|adjust_boost|edit_keywords_hint; schema validation failure or diff mismatch -> retry once (counts against budget), then proposal_status=failed with the rollup still succeeded
rubric_revision_proposals.statuses: pending_review | accepted | rejected | superseded | expired; a newly stored proposal supersedes any leftover pending one; pending older than 30 days is expired by the weekly job
rubric_revision_proposals.review: POST review accept atomically registers a recommendation_rubric_compiles row (model_called=false, prompt_version=revision_proposal_v1) and runs the existing activate chain (rubric_version += 1, audit workspace.recommendation_rubric.activate) then marks the proposal accepted with compile_fingerprint backfilled; reject only flips status and audits; both audit workspace.rubric_revision_proposal.review
rubric_revision_proposals.stale_guard: accept answers 422 when base_rubric_version != current rubric_version (rubric changed since generation) or when the proposal is not pending_review
rubric_revision_proposals.hard_rule: a pending/rejected/expired proposal has zero effect on the active rubric, effective weights or any recommendation run

exploration.behavior: selection layer only, after the current algorithm: when epsilon>0 and the latest weekly rollup has a non-empty low-data source list, a deterministic draw (sha256(run_key + ':exploration') scaled to 0..1) < epsilon reserves at most 1 slot per run for the highest-coarse_score unselected candidate from a low-data source with admission in (P1, P2)
exploration.constraints: never exceeds run limit / source cap / pool cap (conflict abandons the slot); never changes admission; R/P3 never enter via exploration; recommendation_reason appends exploration_slot for explainability
exploration.regression: epsilon=0 keeps selection bit-for-bit identical to current behavior; the draw is reproducible for a fixed run_key

budget.purpose_bucket: feedback_rollup (new enum value on generation_daily_usage.purpose; the String column needs no migration)
budget.cap: fixed 4 calls per workspace per day, not configurable; covers proposal generation plus its single retry; monthly review makes zero LLM calls
budget.isolation: decision locked: do NOT reuse the rubric_compile bucket (that is the interactive compile quota of 20/day; an automated weekly job must not crowd out user actions); the now-four purpose buckets never consume each other's quota; exhaustion -> proposal_status=skipped_budget with the rollup still succeeding

data_model_deltas.migration: one Alembic revision assigned at implementation time, chained after the then-current e/f head (f2b3c4d5e6a7 as of design sign-off)
new_tables: feedback_rollups(id, workspace_code idx, period_type weekly|monthly, period_key, window_start, window_end, status succeeded|empty|failed, proposal_status, metrics_json, source_breakdown_json, topic_breakdown_json, sample_refs_json, computed_at, created_at, updated_at; unique(workspace_code, period_type, period_key); no SyncMixin — never enters sync feeds)

rubric_revision_proposals(id, workspace_code idx, rollup_id fk, base_rubric_version, prompt_version, proposed_rubric_json, change_summary_json, sample_refs_json, status idx(workspace_code,status), review_comment, reviewed_by fk users nullable, reviewed_at, compile_fingerprint, created_at, updated_at; no SyncMixin)
data_model_deltas.report_storage_decision: evaluation reports live in feedback_rollups.metrics_json (the rollup row IS the report); no separate report table

api.list_rollups: GET /api/workspaces/{code}/feedback-rollups?period_type=weekly|monthly&limit=8 (workspace admin+; period_key desc; items carry id/period_type/period_key/window/status/proposal_status/metrics/computed_at)
api.manual_run: POST /api/workspaces/{code}/feedback-rollups/run body {period_type, period_key?=previous complete period} (workspace admin+; synchronous, idempotent overwrite, audited workspace.feedback_rollup.manual_run)
api.review_proposal: POST /api/workspaces/{code}/rubric-revision-proposals/{id}/review body {action: accept|reject, comment} (workspace admin+; accept runs the existing activate chain atomically; 422 on stale/non-pending)

invariants: authored rubric/guidance change only through human-reviewed accept via the existing compile+activate chain
weekly/monthly layers never write source_score_snapshots, rubric_topic_priors or recommendation_items; daily formulas and hard bounds stay untouched
company_sql output and adoption_status semantics are byte-for-byte unaffected; rollups and proposals never enter company SQL or sync feeds
LLM calls run in the dedicated purpose=feedback_rollup bucket (fixed 4/day); the four purpose buckets never crowd each other
rollup jobs are read-only over fact tables: no source enable/tier mutation, no comment/notification/strategy-loop writes, no historical snapshot rewrites
instances without ingestion capability never dispatch rollup jobs; every degradation path (no data / no provider / no budget) completes the rollup instead of failing it
acceptance_assertions: rollup_empty_ok: with zero feedback data the weekly rollup completes, writes a status=empty row with all sample-less metrics null (never 0.0), makes zero LLM calls and creates no proposal
rollup_idempotent: rerunning the same (workspace, period_type, period_key) overwrites the same row without duplicates and never regenerates a proposal for a window that already has one
pending_proposal_no_effect: while a proposal is pending_review, active_rubric/rubric_version/effective weights are unchanged and recommendation runs behave bit-for-bit as without the proposal

accept_uses_activate_chain: accept bumps rubric_version by 1, leaves a model_called=false compile record, and writes both workspace.recommendation_rubric.activate and workspace.rubric_revision_proposal.review audits; no code path ever writes authored rubric fields directly

stale_proposal_guard: accept on base_rubric_version != current rubric_version answers 422; reject leaves policy untouched; a new proposal supersedes leftover pending ones; pending older than 30 days becomes expired

budget_bucket_isolated: proposal generation (including retry) increments only generation_daily_usage purpose=feedback_rollup; the fixed 4/day cap exhaustion yields proposal_status=skipped_budget with the rollup still succeeded

exploration_bounded: exploration_epsilon outside 0..0.1 answers 422 on PATCH; at most 1 exploration slot per run, restricted to admission P1/P2 with recommendation_reason containing exploration_slot; epsilon=0 (default) keeps selection identical to current behavior; the draw is reproducible per run_key

metrics_correct: on a fixed fixture precision_at_6/12, rerank_uplift, source_coverage, topic_entropy and normalized_adopt_rate match hand-computed values; precision is null (not 0) when no published reports exist
position_bias_applied: identical adoption facts at different display ranks produce different normalized_adopt_rate per the fixed bucket weights 1.0/1.2/1.4

monthly_review_advisory: a constructed declining series yields drift_flag=true; constructed stale sources appear in stale_source_suggestions; data_sources.enabled and tier are field-for-field unchanged after the job

no_boundary_leak: sync feed payloads and company SQL exports contain no feedback_rollups/rubric_revision_proposals data; company_sql output is byte-for-byte unchanged; historical recommendation_items/source_score_snapshots/rubric_topic_priors rows are untouched by rollup runs
manual_run_gated: POST feedback-rollups/run is idempotent, audited as workspace.feedback_rollup.manual_run, and answers 403 for viewer/member

dispatch_idempotent_and_gated: one dispatch per trigger point across scheduler instances (heartbeat dedup); capability_ingestion=false instances dispatch nothing; a single workspace failure aborts neither other workspaces nor the scheduler tick

ui_empty_state: with no rollup the settings area and evaluation card render an explicit empty state; null metrics are hidden entirely and the pages never show a 0.0 placeholder (frontend spec guarded)

api.read_policy: GET /api/workspaces/{code}/recommendation-policy (workspace viewer+; response adds resolved {llm_rerank_available, provider_usable, rerank_calls_used_today, rerank_budget, active_rubric_version, semantic_layer_available}; never any key material)
api.update_policy: PATCH /api/workspaces/{code}/recommendation-policy (workspace admin+ or super_admin; 422 on range/secret-like violations; audited)

secrets_boundary.rerank_and_compile: reuse the unified generation provider resolution chain via resolve_generation_config (workspace generation_policy.credential_id credential row -> GENERATION_* instance env; see llm_providers.json resolution_priority; degrades to the pure env chain before D-2026-07-08-KEY lands). The recommendation module introduces no new secret fields; plaintext keys never enter recommendation_policy, Git, sync feeds or API responses (at-rest keys only as llm_provider_credentials Fernet ciphertext governed by R2); credential disabled/undecryptable counts as provider unusable (skip_reason=provider_unavailable)
secrets_boundary.embedding: EMBEDDING_API_KEY(_REF) is instance env only, same credential_ref/fail-fast/redaction rules as GENERATION_API_KEY; disabled by default
secrets_boundary.guidance: guidance text is business configuration, not a secret, but all policy payloads pass secret-like detection (422 on hit) and guidance/rubric never enter company SQL or exports

acceptance_assertions: baseline_no_guidance: with recommendation_policy absent or llm_rerank_enabled=false, recommendation_items rank sequence and final_score on a fixed fixture are identical to the current rule-only implementation

budget_exhausted_degrade: when the rerank budget is exhausted the run completes, llm_rerank.status in (skipped, partial) with skip_reason=budget_exhausted, and non-reranked items keep final_score=coarse_score
provider_down_degrade: with provider unusable there are zero outbound calls, llm_rerank.status=skipped(provider_unavailable), and the run completes

rubric_compile_idempotent_preview: compiling identical guidance twice returns the same fingerprint and rubric, the second call makes zero model calls (cache hit), and active_rubric/rubric_version are unchanged

rubric_activate_versioned_audited: activation bumps rubric_version by 1 and writes audit action workspace.recommendation_rubric.activate with before/after version+fingerprint; unknown or expired fingerprints answer 422
window_deterministic: for a fixed run_key the window partition, anchors and in-window shuffled order are exactly reproducible

window_failed_fallback: a window failing after one retry marks its items llm_rerank_status=window_failed with final_score=coarse_score; more than half failed windows marks the run llm_rerank.status=failed with full fallback
fusion_formula: scored items satisfy final_score = round(0.6*llm_relevance_score + 0.4*coarse_score, 2) under default weights; PATCH with fusion weights not summing to 1.0 answers 422
ordering_consistency: dashboard headline candidates, candidate pool default sort, daily draft sort_order and today-overview cards all order by final_score desc, guarded by frontend specs
empty_metric_hidden: rating_count=0 renders no average-rating metric (no '0.0 平均评分'); missing final_score renders 未评分 instead of 0.0

rerank_never_changes_admission: L3 leaves admission_level/admission_pool/noise_types/reject_reasons untouched, never writes raw/news/dedupe, and R/P3 items never enter the rerank input set (caps 25/44 preserved)

feedback_reestimate_bounded: source_prior_delta stays within [-6,6], effective topic weights within [0.5w,1.5w], daily re-estimation is a full recompute, and authored rubric weights are never rewritten
budget_buckets_isolated: rerank calls increment generation_daily_usage purpose=rerank without touching purpose=generation counts, and vice versa; rubric_compile is capped at 20/day
secrets_boundary: recommendation-policy endpoints answer 422 on secret-like payloads, responses contain no key material, and no new secret fields are introduced by rerank/compile

existing_guarantees_hold: runs only process target-day active winners; admission, score breakdowns and reject reasons are persisted; candidates trace news_items -> raw_items -> data_sources; source daily limit is enforced with recorded reasons; old run snapshots are never overwritten by new feedback; workspace policies never cross-contaminate; scorer-policy/scorer-preview/observation-pool-review v1 behavior is unchanged
